prose.rule: DELIVERY is the ONE moment an agent crosses from inside the digital world to the outside real world. THE SCREEN IS A PORTAL between the two worlds. It is literalized as the Chief of Agents coming HALF-WAY THROUGH the laptop screen-portal: its lower body and legs stay INSIDE / behind the glowing screen surface (still in the digital world), and only its upper half (chest, arms, hatted head, holding the finished work) leans OUT through the plane of the screen into the real room to hand it to the human. The screen surface cuts across its middle with a soft glowing light-seam where its body passes through. It must read as reaching THROUGH the portal, NEVER as standing fully outside on the laptop. The crossing happens exactly ONCE per delivery; nowhere else does a digital being enter the real world. THE WORK PRODUCT ITSELF BECOMES REAL AT THE CROSSING: the delivered artifact (a bound report, a document, the finished thing) is rendered SOLID, opaque, physical, and casts a real shadow like any real-world object — never holographic, glowing, or orange. The Chief that hands it over is digital and glowing; the work it delivers has landed in the human world and is real. That contrast is the point of the crossing.
prose.source: Locked with Gary 2026-07-16 (chief-delivering-v4, then the isolated delivering.png).
